reasoning: The FRED trend analysis tool gives a combined estimate of 0.65 for a yield increase, acting as our primary anchor. It suggests that despite a recent negative trend (yield has fallen from 6.66 on Jan 8 to 6.58 on Jan 16), the regression fit suggests the short-term mean reversion favors an increase over the initial 6.58 threshold. For the initial date (Jan 25, one week away), I am placing the probability slightly below the anchor at 62%, because bond yields often act more as a random walk than a mean-reverting series over short periods. As per the instructions, our uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, pulling the probability for further dates closer to 0.50. Thus, for the later resolution date (Feb 17, ~1 month away), the probability is centered much closer to neutral at 54%, reflecting the increased uncertainty and the potential for new macroeconomic developments (like an end-of-January FOMC decision) to offset the simple mean reversion.
